name: Sugar Maple
description: A tall deciduous tree known for its brilliant orange and red fall leaves. Sugar Maple is famous for producing sap used to make maple syrup and provides dense shade in forests and parks.

name: Shagbark Hickory
description: A large hardwood tree recognized by its peeling shaggy bark. It produces edible nuts that are important food sources for wildlife such as squirrels, deer, and birds.

name: White Pine
description: A fast-growing evergreen tree with soft blue-green needles grouped in bundles of five. White Pine is often used for timber, windbreaks, and habitat for birds.

name: Ginseng
description: A slow-growing woodland herb valued for its forked root, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. It grows best in shaded forests with rich soil.

name: Pink Turtlehead
description: A native perennial flower with pink blossoms shaped like a turtle's head. It thrives in moist soils and attracts bees, butterflies, and hummingbirds.

name: Big Bluestem
description: A tall prairie grass often called the king of the prairie. It can grow over six feet tall and provides shelter and food for many prairie animals.

name: Switchgrass
description: A hardy native grass with upright stems and airy seed heads. Switchgrass is valuable for erosion control, wildlife habitat, and renewable biofuel research.

name: Common Milkweed
description: A flowering plant with clusters of pink-purple blooms and milky sap. It is one of the most important host plants for Monarch butterfly caterpillars.

name: Black-eyed Susan
description: A cheerful wildflower with bright yellow petals surrounding a dark brown center. It blooms in summer and attracts bees, butterflies, and other pollinators.

name: Purple Coneflower
description: A native prairie flower with purple petals and a spiky orange cone center. It is popular in gardens and often associated with herbal remedies.

name: Longhorn Cattle
description: A hardy breed of cattle known for their long curved horns and strong grazing ability. Longhorn cattle are valued for conservation grazing and historical significance.

name: Ossabaw Island Hog
description: A rare heritage breed descended from pigs brought to North America centuries ago. These hogs are known for their adaptability and ability to forage naturally.

name: Tunis Sheep
description: A medium-sized heritage sheep breed recognized by its reddish face and legs. Tunis sheep are calm animals prized for meat, wool, and easy handling.

name: Arapawa Goat
description: A rare goat breed believed to descend from old English goats. Arapawa goats are agile browsers with distinctive horns and are valued for genetic conservation.
